title: JointInput Object
description: Defines all of the information required to create a new joint. This object provides equivalent functionality to the Joint command dialog in that it gathers the required information to create a joint.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: setAsBallJointMotion
methods.description: Defines the relationship between the two joint geometries as a ball joint.

methods.name: setAsCylindricalJointMotion
methods.description: Defines the relationship between the two joint geometries as a cylindrical joint.

methods.name: setAsPinSlotJointMotion
methods.description: Defines the relationship between the two joint geometries as a pin-slot joint.

methods.name: setAsPlanarJointMotion
methods.description: Defines the relationship between the two joint geometries as a planar joint.

methods.name: setAsRevoluteJointMotion
methods.description: Defines the relationship between the two joint geometries as a revolute joint.

methods.name: setAsRigidJointMotion
methods.description: Defines the relationship between the two joint geometries as a rigid joint.

methods.name: setAsSliderJointMotion
methods.description: Defines the relationship between the two joint geometries as a slider joint.

properties.name: angle
properties.description: Specifies the angle between two input geometries. This is effectively the angle between the two primary axes of the input geometries. When a new JointInput object is created, this value defaults to zero. When the joint is created this will become the value of the parameter that controls the joint angle.   When using a real value to define the angle, the value is in radians. When using a string the expression is evaluated using the document default units for angles.

properties.name: geometryOrOriginOne
properties.description: Gets and sets the first JointGeometry or JointOrigin for this joint.

properties.name: geometryOrOriginTwo
properties.description: Gets and sets the second JointGeometry or JointOrigin for this joint.

properties.name: isFlipped
properties.description: Gets and sets if the joint direction is flipped or not. This is effectively specifying if the third axis of the two input geometries is facing (false) or opposed (true).

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: jointMotion
properties.description: Returns an object derived from JointMotion that defines how the motion between the two joint geometries is defined.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: offset
properties.description: Specifies the offset between two input geometries. This is effectively the offset distance between the two planes defined by the primary and secondary axes of the input geometries. When a new JointInput object is created, this value defaults to zero. When the joint is created this will become the value of the parameter that controls the joint offset.   When using a real value to define the offset, the value is in centimeters. When using a string the expression is evaluated using the document default units for distance.

related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a joint with ball joint motion

related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a joint with cylindrical joint motion.

angle.title: JointInput.angle Property
angle.description: Specifies the angle between two input geometries. This is effectively the angle between the two primary axes of the input geometries. When a new JointInput object is created, this value defaults to zero. When the joint is created this will become the value of the parameter that controls the joint angle. When using a real value to define the angle, the value is in radians. When using a string the expression is evaluated using the document default units for angles.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = jointInput_var.angle

# Set the value of the property.
jointInput_var.angle = propertyValue

classType.title: JointInput.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

setAsBallJointMotion.title: JointInput.setAsBallJointMotion Method
setAsBallJointMotion.description: Defines the relationship between the two joint geometries as a ball joint.

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = jointInput_var.setAsBallJointMotion(pitchDirection, yawDirection)

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = jointInput_var.setAsBallJointMotion(pitchDirection, yawDirection, customPitchDirection, customYawDirection)

setAsBallJointMotion.return_description: Returns true if the operation was successful.

parameters.name: pitchDirection
parameters.description: Defines the direction the pitch angle is measured from. This can be ZAxisJointDirection or CustomJointDirection. If CustomJointDirection is specified then you must also provide a value for the customPitchDirection argument.

parameters.name: yawDirection
parameters.description: Defines the direction the yaw is measured from. This can be XAxisJointDirection or CustomJointDirection. If CustomJointDirection is specified then you must also provide a value for the customYawDirection argument.

parameters.name: customPitchDirection
parameters.description: If the pitchDirection argument is customPitchDirection this argument is used to define the direction the pitch angel is measured from. This can be several types of entities that can define a direction.   This is an optional argument whose default value is null.

parameters.name: customYawDirection
parameters.description: If the yawDirection argument is customPitchDirection this argument is used to define the direction the yaw angel is measured from. This can be several types of entities that can define a direction.   This is an optional argument whose default value is null.

setAsCylindricalJointMotion.title: JointInput.setAsCylindricalJointMotion Method
setAsCylindricalJointMotion.description: Defines the relationship between the two joint geometries as a cylindrical joint.

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = jointInput_var.setAsCylindricalJointMotion(rotationAxis)

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = jointInput_var.setAsCylindricalJointMotion(rotationAxis, customRotationAxisEntity)

setAsCylindricalJointMotion.return_description: Returns true if the operation was successful.

parameters.name: rotationAxis
parameters.description: Specifies which axis the rotation is around. If this is set to CustomJointDirection then the customRotationAxisEntity argument must also be provided.

parameters.name: customRotationAxisEntity
parameters.description: If the rotationAxis is customAxisEntity this argument is used to specify the entity that defines the custom axis of rotation. This can be several types of entities that an axis can be derived from.   This is an optional argument whose default value is null.

related_samples.name: CylindricalJointMotion API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a joint with cylindrical joint motion.

setAsPinSlotJointMotion.title: JointInput.setAsPinSlotJointMotion Method
setAsPinSlotJointMotion.description: Defines the relationship between the two joint geometries as a pin-slot joint.

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = jointInput_var.setAsPinSlotJointMotion(rotationAxis, slideDirection)

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = jointInput_var.setAsPinSlotJointMotion(rotationAxis, slideDirection, customRotationAxisEntity, customSlideDirectionEntity)

setAsPinSlotJointMotion.return_description: Returns true if the operation was successful.

parameters.name: rotationAxis
parameters.description: Specifies which axis the rotation is around. If this is set to CustomJointDirection then the customRotationAxisEntity argument must also be provided.

parameters.name: slideDirection
parameters.description: Specifies which axis the slide direction is along. If this is set to CustomJointDirection then the customSlideDirectionEntity argument must also be provided.

parameters.name: customRotationAxisEntity
parameters.description: If the rotationAxis is customAxisEntity this argument is used to specify the entity that defines the custom axis of rotation. This can be several types of entities that an axis can be derived   This is an optional argument whose default value is null.

parameters.name: customSlideDirectionEntity
parameters.description: If the slideDirection is CustomJointDirection this argument is used to specify the entity that defines the custom slide direction. This can be several types of entities that can define a direction.   This is an optional argument whose default value is null.

setAsPlanarJointMotion.title: JointInput.setAsPlanarJointMotion Method
setAsPlanarJointMotion.description: Defines the relationship between the two joint geometries as a planar joint.

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = jointInput_var.setAsPlanarJointMotion(normalDirection)

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = jointInput_var.setAsPlanarJointMotion(normalDirection, customNormalDirectionEntity, customPrimarySlideDirection)

setAsPlanarJointMotion.return_description: Returns true if the operation was successful.

parameters.name: normalDirection
parameters.description: Defines the direction of the normal of the single degree of rotation. This can be set to XAxisJointDirection, YAxisJointDirection, ZAxisJointDirection, or CustomJointDirection. If set to CustomJointDirection then the customNormalDirectionEntity argument must also be provided.

parameters.name: customNormalDirectionEntity
parameters.description: If the normalDirection is CustomJointDirection this argument is used to specify the entity that defines the direction of the normal. This can be several types of entities that can define a direction.   This is an optional argument whose default value is null.

parameters.name: customPrimarySlideDirection
parameters.description: This arguments defines the direction of the primary slide direction. A default primary slide direction is automatically chosen and will be used if this argument is not provided or is null. The secondary slide direction is automatically inferred from the normal and primary slide directions.   This is an optional argument whose default value is null.

setAsRevoluteJointMotion.title: JointInput.setAsRevoluteJointMotion Method
setAsRevoluteJointMotion.description: Defines the relationship between the two joint geometries as a revolute joint.

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = jointInput_var.setAsRevoluteJointMotion(rotationAxis)

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = jointInput_var.setAsRevoluteJointMotion(rotationAxis, customRotationAxisEntity)

setAsRevoluteJointMotion.return_description: Returns true if the operation was successful.

parameters.name: rotationAxis
parameters.description: Specifies which axis the rotation is around. If this is set to CustomJointDirection then the customRotationAxisEntity argument must also be provided.

parameters.name: customRotationAxisEntity
parameters.description: If the rotationAxis is customAxisEntity this argument is used to specify the entity that defines the custom axis of rotation. This can be several types of entities that an axis can be derived from.   This is an optional argument whose default value is null.

setAsSliderJointMotion.title: JointInput.setAsSliderJointMotion Method
setAsSliderJointMotion.description: Defines the relationship between the two joint geometries as a slider joint.

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = jointInput_var.setAsSliderJointMotion(sliderDirection)

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = jointInput_var.setAsSliderJointMotion(sliderDirection, customSliderDirectionEntity)

setAsSliderJointMotion.return_description: Returns true if the operation was successful.

parameters.name: sliderDirection
parameters.description: Specifies which axis the slide direction is along. If this is set to CustomJointDirection then the customSliderDirectionEntity argument must also be provided.

parameters.name: customSliderDirectionEntity
parameters.description: If the sliderDirection is CustomJointDirection this argument is used to specify the entity that defines the custom slider direction. This can be several types of entities that can define a direction.   This is an optional argument whose default value is null.
